steps.title: QR code at point of sale
steps.description: Sign at checkout: 'Join the list — get 15% off your next visit.' Converts 20-40% of customers.

steps.title: Receipt opt-in
steps.description: POS checkbox at checkout. Adds 10-20% more on top of the QR sign.

steps.title: Staff prompt
steps.description: Train counter staff to ask: 'Want to join our list for specials?'

steps.title: GBP link
steps.description: Add your signup form to your Google Business Profile quick-action.

steps.title: Targeted Facebook lead form
steps.description: $50-100 to a 3-mile radius. Picks up 30-50 emails.

assets.reason: Phone support included from $12/month, 97.4% inbox placement vs 96.2%, predictable pricing as you scale, and a 60-day free trial. Wins for the typical small business owner.

assets.prompt: Editorial split-frame composition: on the left, a small business owner in a comfortable shop interior phoning customer support with relief, warm tone; on the right, a marketing professional in a modern open-plan office surrounded by multiple monitors showing flow diagrams (text illegible), cool tone. assets.winner: Constant Contact for typical small business
assets.reason: ActiveCampaign wins on automation depth — but most small businesses use 5-10% of its capability. For owner-operated, weekly-cadence senders, Constant Contact's mix of price + ease + included phone support wins.

assets.prompt: Editorial split-frame composition: on the left, a small business owner with employees visible at a real shop counter reviewing analytics on a laptop; on the right, a solo content creator in a sunlit home office writing in a notebook beside a single laptop. Editorial photography, balanced and human. No text or logos. 16:9.

assets.title: When MailerLite wins vs when Constant Contact wins
pros: Solopreneur or one-person blogger? MailerLite's clean UI + free tier wins.
Side hustle on a tight budget? MailerLite at $10 entry wins.
Design-savvy creator? MailerLite's templates feel more modern.
cons: Real business with employees? Constant Contact wins (phone support).
Local service biz with events? Constant Contact (events tool).
Importing a messy customer list? Constant Contact (list-import forgiveness).
